---
title: Filters
---

::: {.callout-tip}
Click the `</>` button in the top navigation bar to view the original `.qmd` source code.
:::

Filters handle fenced divs and bracketed spans that need logic beyond `{{variable}}` substitution: conditional rendering, computed values, content rewriting.

Filters are implemented as plugins. Each plugin declares which elements it handles via match rules in its `plugin.yml` manifest.


# Writing a filter plugin

A filter plugin is a directory with a `plugin.yml` and an executable script. The `match` section declares exactly which elements trigger the filter.

## Match rules

Match fields are OR'd: any match triggers the filter.

- `classes` --- CSS classes. The filter runs when any listed class is present.
- `attrs` --- attribute names. The filter runs when any listed attribute is present.
- `id_prefix` --- a string prefix. The filter runs when the element's id starts with this prefix.
- `formats` --- restrict to specific output formats. Omit to handle all formats.

## Input protocol

The executable receives flat JSON on stdin.

For a div:

```json
{
  "context": "div",
  "content": "<p>rendered children</p>",
  "classes": ["myclass"],
  "id": "my-id",
  "format": "html",
  "title": "user attribute"
}
```

For a span:

```json
{
  "context": "span",
  "content": "span text",
  "classes": ["myclass"],
  "id": "",
  "format": "html",
  "tip": "user attribute"
}
```

User attributes from the element are flattened into the top-level JSON object.

## Output protocol

- Exit 0 with output on stdout: the output replaces the element (template is skipped).
- Exit non-zero or empty stdout: falls through to next handler.


# Example: a div filter

`_calepin/plugins/accordion/plugin.yml`:

```yaml
name: accordion
version: 0.1.0
description: "Collapsible accordion sections"

provides:
  filter:
    run: filter.py
    match:
      classes: [accordion]
    contexts: [div]
```

`_calepin/plugins/accordion/filter.py`:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, sys

data = json.load(sys.stdin)
title = data.get("title", "Details")
content = data["content"]
fmt = data["format"]

if fmt == "html":
    print(f'<details><summary>{title}</summary>{content}</details>')
elif fmt == "tex":
    print(f'\\paragraph{{{title}}} {content}')
else:
    print(f'**{title}**\n\n{content}')
```

Usage:

    ::: {.accordion title="Click to expand"}
    Hidden content here.
    :::


# Example: a span filter

`_calepin/plugins/tooltip/plugin.yml`:

```yaml
name: tooltip
version: 0.1.0
description: "Tooltip on hover"

provides:
  filter:
    run: filter.py
    match:
      classes: [tooltip]
    contexts: [span]
```

`_calepin/plugins/tooltip/filter.py`:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, sys

data = json.load(sys.stdin)
text = data["content"]
tip = data.get("tip", "")
fmt = data["format"]

if fmt == "html":
    print(f'<span class="tooltip" title="{tip}">{text}</span>')
else:
    print(text)
```

Usage: `This has [hover me]{.tooltip tip="Extra info"} inline.`


# Example: wordcount filter

`_calepin/plugins/wordcount/plugin.yml`:

```yaml
name: wordcount
version: 0.1.0
description: "Word count badge for div content"

provides:
  filter:
    run: filter.py
    match:
      classes: [wordcount]
    contexts: [div]
```

`_calepin/plugins/wordcount/filter.py`:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, sys, re

data = json.load(sys.stdin)
content = data["content"]
plain = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", content)
count = len(plain.split())

badge = f'<p style="font-size: 0.85em; color: #666;">({count} words)</p>'
print(f'<div class="wordcount">{badge}\n{content}</div>')
```


# Built-in filters

These are compiled into *Calepin* and registered as built-in plugins. They run after user plugins, so you can override them by creating a plugin with the same match rules.

## TheoremFilter

Handles theorem-type classes and maintains per-type counters. See [Math](math.qmd#theorem-environments) for the full list of supported types, cross-reference prefixes, and template customization.

Matches: `.theorem`, `.lemma`, `.corollary`, `.proposition`, `.conjecture`, `.definition`, `.example`, `.exercise`, `.solution`, `.remark`, `.algorithm`, `.proof`.

## CalloutFilter

Enriches `callout-note`, `callout-tip`, `callout-warning`, `callout-important`, and `callout-caution` divs with `{{title}}`, `{{icon}}`, `{{collapse}}`, and `{{appearance}}` variables.


# Processing order

When *Calepin* encounters a fenced div or bracketed span, it iterates over all plugins in registry order (user plugins first, then built-in):

1. For each plugin whose `match` rules apply to the element:
   - **Structural plugins** (tabset, layout, figure-div): receive raw children, return if handled.
   - **Filter plugins**: receive rendered children, return if output produced.
   - **Subprocess plugins**: receive JSON, return if stdout is non-empty.
2. **Template** (by class name, then `div.{ext}`)
3. **Default fallback** (generic markup)

A handler that produces output short-circuits the pipeline. Filters returning `Continue` (like TheoremFilter and CalloutFilter) enrich template variables and continue to the next handler.


# Built-in div classes

## `.hidden`

Processes content (R code executes, variables are set) but produces no output:

::: {.hidden}
```{r}
hidden_value <- 99
```
:::

The variable is available: `{r} hidden_value`.


# Cross-references

See [Math](math.qmd#cross-references) for theorem cross-reference prefixes and label resolution.
